speaker: RICHARD
text: Stand all apart.—Cousin of Buckingham.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: My gracious sovereign.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Give me thy hand.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Thus high, by thy advice

speaker: RICHARD
text: And thy assistance is King Richard seated.

speaker: RICHARD
text: But shall we wear these glories for a day,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Or shall they last and we rejoice in them?

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Still live they, and forever let them last.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Ah, Buckingham, now do I play the touch,

speaker: RICHARD
text: To try if thou be current gold indeed:

speaker: RICHARD
text: Young Edward lives; think now what I would speak.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Say on, my loving lord.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Why, Buckingham, I say I would be king.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Why so you are, my thrice-renownèd lord.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Ha! Am I king? ’Tis so—but Edward lives.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: True, noble prince.

speaker: RICHARD
text: O bitter consequence

speaker: RICHARD
text: That Edward still should live “true noble prince”!

speaker: RICHARD
text: Cousin, thou wast not wont to be so dull.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Shall I be plain? I wish the bastards dead,

speaker: RICHARD
text: And I would have it suddenly performed.

speaker: RICHARD
text: What sayst thou now? Speak suddenly. Be brief.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Your Grace may do your pleasure.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Tut, tut, thou art all ice; thy kindness freezes.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Say, have I thy consent that they shall die?

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Give me some little breath, some pause, dear lord,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Before I positively speak in this.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: I will resolve you herein presently.

speaker: CATESBY
text: The King is angry. See, he gnaws his lip.

speaker: RICHARD
text: I will converse with iron-witted fools

speaker: RICHARD
text: And unrespective boys. None are for me

speaker: RICHARD
text: That look into me with considerate eyes.

speaker: RICHARD
text: High-reaching Buckingham grows circumspect.—

speaker: RICHARD
text: Boy!

speaker: PAGE
text: My lord?

speaker: RICHARD
text: Know’st thou not any whom corrupting gold

speaker: RICHARD
text: Will tempt unto a close exploit of death?

speaker: PAGE
text: I know a discontented gentleman

speaker: PAGE
text: Whose humble means match not his haughty spirit.

speaker: PAGE
text: Gold were as good as twenty orators,

speaker: PAGE
text: And will, no doubt, tempt him to anything.

speaker: RICHARD
text: What is his name?

speaker: PAGE
text: His name, my lord, is Tyrrel.

speaker: RICHARD
text: I partly know the man. Go, call him hither, boy.

speaker: RICHARD
text:
Aside
                .
                 
              The deep-revolving witty Buckingham

speaker: RICHARD
text: No more shall be the neighbor to my counsels.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Hath he so long held out with me, untired,

speaker: RICHARD
text: And stops he now for breath? Well, be it so.

speaker: RICHARD
text: How now, Lord Stanley, what’s the news?

speaker: STANLEY
text: Know, my loving lord,

speaker: STANLEY
text: The Marquess Dorset, as I hear, is fled

speaker: STANLEY
text: To Richmond, in the parts where he abides.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Come hither, Catesby. Rumor it abroad

speaker: RICHARD
text: That Anne my wife is very grievous sick.

speaker: RICHARD
text: I will take order for her keeping close.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Inquire me out some mean poor gentleman,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Whom I will marry straight to Clarence’ daughter.

speaker: RICHARD
text: The boy is foolish, and I fear not him.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Look how thou dream’st! I say again, give out

speaker: RICHARD
text: That Anne my queen is sick and like to die.

speaker: RICHARD
text: About it, for it stands me much upon

speaker: RICHARD
text: To stop all hopes whose growth may damage me.

speaker: RICHARD
text:
Aside
                .
                 
              I must be married to my brother’s daughter,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Or else my kingdom stands on brittle glass.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Murder her brothers, and then marry her—

speaker: RICHARD
text: Uncertain way of gain. But I am in

speaker: RICHARD
text: So far in blood that sin will pluck on sin.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Tear-falling pity dwells not in this eye.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Is thy name Tyrrel?

speaker: TYRREL
text: James Tyrrel, and your most obedient subject.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Art thou indeed?

speaker: TYRREL
text: Prove me, my gracious lord.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Dar’st thou resolve to kill a friend of mine?

speaker: TYRREL
text: Please you. But I had rather kill two enemies.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Why then, thou hast it. Two deep enemies,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Foes to my rest, and my sweet sleep’s disturbers,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Are they that I would have thee deal upon.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Tyrrel, I mean those bastards in the Tower.

speaker: TYRREL
text: Let me have open means to come to them,

speaker: TYRREL
text: And soon I’ll rid you from the fear of them.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Thou sing’st sweet music. Hark, come hither, Tyrrel.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Go, by this token. Rise, and lend thine ear.

speaker: RICHARD
text: There is no more but so. Say it is done,

speaker: RICHARD
text: And I will love thee and prefer thee for it.

speaker: TYRREL
text: I will dispatch it straight.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: My lord, I have considered in my mind

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: The late request that you did sound me in.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Well, let that rest. Dorset is fled to Richmond.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: I hear the news, my lord.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Stanley, he is your wife’s son. Well, look unto it.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: My lord, I claim the gift, my due by promise,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: For which your honor and your faith is pawned—

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Th’ earldom of Hereford and the movables

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Which you have promisèd I shall possess.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Stanley, look to your wife. If she convey

speaker: RICHARD
text: Letters to Richmond, you shall answer it.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: What says your Highness to my just request?

speaker: RICHARD
text: I do remember me, Henry the Sixth

speaker: RICHARD
text: Did prophesy that Richmond should be king,

speaker: RICHARD
text: When Richmond was a little peevish boy.

speaker: RICHARD
text: A king perhaps—

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: My lord—

speaker: RICHARD
text: How chance the prophet could not at that time

speaker: RICHARD
text: Have told me, I being by, that I should kill him?

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: My lord, your promise for the earldom—

speaker: RICHARD
text: Richmond! When last I was at Exeter,

speaker: RICHARD
text: The Mayor in courtesy showed me the castle

speaker: RICHARD
text: And called it Rougemont, at which name I started,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Because a bard of Ireland told me once

speaker: RICHARD
text: I should not live long after I saw Richmond.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: My lord—

speaker: RICHARD
text: Ay, what’s o’clock?

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: I am thus bold to put your Grace in mind

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Of what you promised me.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Well, but what’s o’clock?

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Upon the stroke of ten.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Well, let it strike.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Why let it strike?

speaker: RICHARD
text: Because that, like a jack, thou keep’st the stroke

speaker: RICHARD
text: Betwixt thy begging and my meditation.

speaker: RICHARD
text: I am not in the giving vein today.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Why then, resolve me whether you will or no.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Thou troublest me; I am not in the vein.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: And is it thus? Repays he my deep service

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: With such contempt? Made I him king for this?

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: O, let me think on Hastings and be gone

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: To Brecknock, while my fearful head is on!
